summary: In renal osteodystrophy, impaired kidney function causes retention of phosphate leading to elevated serum phosphorus (**hyperphosphatemia**) whereas nutritional and genetic forms—like X-linked hypophosphatemic rickets—are associated with decreased phosphate absorption resulting in **hypophosphatemia**. This difference in serum phosphate levels serves as a key diagnostic distinction between these two groups of disorders.
